data_cap_payment.label: Védi a payment correctnesset
data_cap_payment.description: Critical payment write-nál inkább reject vagy delay, mint double-spend vagy conflicting state.

data_cap_dashboard.label: Elfogad stale dashboard readet
data_cap_dashboard.description: Analytics esetén available, de esetleg stale read elfogadható lehet.

data_cap_mentions_pacelc.label: Megemlíti a PACELC-et
data_cap_mentions_pacelc.description: Partition nélkül is van latency-consistency trade-off, például synchronous replicationnél.

followUps.data_cap_follow_same: Miért túl egyszerű általában egyetlen CP vagy AP címkét adni az egész companyra?

questions.title: Keeping a booking flow consistent across services
questions.prompt: A booking touches inventory, payment, booking, document generation, and loyalty services. How would you keep the flow consistent without a global transaction?
questions.answerStructureHint: Define durable saga state, local transactions, compensation, critical versus non-critical steps, and recovery.

expectedPoints.label: Models explicit saga state
expectedPoints.description: Uses a durable workflow state and step status instead of relying on an in-memory request chain.

expectedPoints.label: Uses local transactions
expectedPoints.description: Each service commits only its own data and publishes or records the next transition reliably.

expectedPoints.label: Defines compensation
expectedPoints.description: Releases inventory or voids payment when a later critical step fails.

expectedPoints.label: Separates critical and non-critical work
expectedPoints.description: Keeps booking correctness in the main saga while document, loyalty, or notification work may retry asynchronously.

expectedPoints.label: Plans recovery and reconciliation
expectedPoints.description: Includes idempotent steps, timeout handling, monitoring, and reconciliation for stuck or ambiguous state.

followUps.prompt: Would you orchestrate or choreograph this saga, and why?

hu.title: Booking flow consistency több service között
hu.prompt: Egy booking érinti az inventory, payment, booking, document generation és loyalty service-eket. Hogyan tartanád konzisztensen global transaction nélkül?
hu.answerStructureHint: Legyen durable saga state, local transaction, compensation, critical és non-critical lépések, valamint recovery.

data_saga_state.label: Explicit saga state-et modellez
data_saga_state.description: Durable workflow state és step status van, nem egy in-memory request chain.

data_saga_local.label: Local transactionöket használ
data_saga_local.description: Minden service csak a saját adatát commitolja, és reliable módon rögzíti vagy publisholja a következő transitiont.

data_saga_comp.label: Compensationt definiál
data_saga_comp.description: Későbbi critical failure esetén inventory release vagy payment void/refund történik.

data_saga_priority.label: Elkülöníti a critical és non-critical munkát
data_saga_priority.description: A booking correctness a fő saga része, document, loyalty vagy notification async retryolható.

data_saga_recover.label: Recoveryt és reconciliationt tervez
data_saga_recover.description: Idempotent step, timeout handling, monitoring és stuck vagy ambiguous state javítás szerepel.

followUps.data_saga_follow_style: Orchestrálnád vagy choreographyn keresztül kezelnéd ezt a sagát, és miért?

questions.title: Reliable event publishing with duplicate delivery
questions.prompt: A service must update a booking and publish BookingCreated. The database commit can succeed while broker publishing fails, and the broker may later redeliver the event. Design the flow.
questions.answerStructureHint: Use one local transaction for business plus outbox, a retriable publisher, and an idempotent consumer.

expectedPoints.label: Writes business and outbox atomically
expectedPoints.description: Stores the booking change and event record in the same local database transaction.

expectedPoints.label: Publishes asynchronously and retries
expectedPoints.description: A worker or CDC process publishes pending events and marks them only after durable broker confirmation.

expectedPoints.label: Makes the consumer idempotent
expectedPoints.description: Uses a stable event ID, unique constraint, processed-events record, UPSERT, or guarded transition.

expectedPoints.label: States the delivery semantics honestly
expectedPoints.description: Explains that outbox plus broker still may deliver duplicates and is not magical exactly-once across systems.

followUps.prompt: How would you preserve ordering for several events belonging to the same booking?

hu.title: Reliable event publish duplicate delivery mellett
hu.prompt: A service update-eli a bookingot és BookingCreated eventet publishol. A database commit sikerülhet, miközben a broker publish elbukik, később pedig a broker duplicate eventet küldhet. Tervezd meg a flow-t.
hu.answerStructureHint: A business change és outbox egy local transaction, a publisher retryolható, a consumer idempotent.

data_outbox_atomic.label: Atomikusan írja a business és outbox adatot
data_outbox_atomic.description: A booking change és event record ugyanabban a local database transactionben kerül mentésre.

data_outbox_publish.label: Asynchronously publishol és retryol
data_outbox_publish.description: Worker vagy CDC publisholja a pending eventet, és csak durable broker confirmation után jelöli publishednek.

data_outbox_consumer.label: Idempotent consumert tervez
data_outbox_consumer.description: Stable event ID, unique constraint, processed-events record, UPSERT vagy guarded transition szerepel.

data_outbox_semantics.label: Őszintén megnevezi a delivery semanticset
data_outbox_semantics.description: Az outbox és broker mellett is lehet duplicate; ez nem mágikus exactly-once systemeken át.

followUps.data_outbox_follow_order: Hogyan őriznéd meg ugyanahhoz a bookinghoz tartozó több event sorrendjét?

questions.title: Renaming a production database column safely
questions.prompt: You need to rename status to state in a table used by several application versions during rolling deployments. How would you migrate without downtime?
questions.answerStructureHint: Use expand-and-contract: add, dual support, backfill, switch reads, verify, and remove later.

expectedPoints.label: Expands with a compatible change
expectedPoints.description: Adds the new state column without removing or requiring the old field immediately.

expectedPoints.label: Supports old and new application versions
expectedPoints.description: Temporarily reads or writes both representations so rolling instances remain compatible.

expectedPoints.label: Backfills safely
expectedPoints.description: Uses monitored, restartable, throttled batches and watches locks and replication lag.

expectedPoints.label: Contracts only after verification
expectedPoints.description: Switches reads, verifies all consumers, and removes the old column in a later release.

followUps.prompt: How would the plan change if you also needed to build a large new index?

hu.title: Production database column biztonságos átnevezése
hu.prompt: A status columnt state-re kellene rename-elni egy olyan table-ben, amelyet rolling deployment közben több application version használ. Hogyan migrálnád downtime nélkül?
hu.answerStructureHint: Expand-and-contract: add, dual support, backfill, read switch, verify, majd későbbi removal.

data_mig_expand.label: Compatible change-dzsel expandel
data_mig_expand.description: Hozzáadja az új state columnt anélkül, hogy azonnal törölné vagy required-dá tenné a régit.

data_mig_dual.label: Támogatja a régi és új application versiont
data_mig_dual.description: Átmenetileg mindkét representationt olvassa vagy írja, ezért a rolling instance-ok kompatibilisek.

data_mig_backfill.label: Biztonságosan backfillel
data_mig_backfill.description: Monitored, restartable és throttled batch-eket használ, figyeli a lockot és replication lagot.

data_mig_contract.label: Csak verification után contractol
data_mig_contract.description: Read switch, consumer verification, majd későbbi release-ben old column removal.

followUps.data_mig_follow_index: Hogyan változna a terv, ha egy nagy új indexet is fel kellene építeni?

questions.title: Reconciling booking and payment state
questions.prompt: The booking database says CONFIRMED, but the payment provider says the payment failed. How would you design reconciliation and decide which side is authoritative?
questions.answerStructureHint: Define authoritative facts and matching keys, detect mismatches, classify safe repair versus manual review, and measure outcomes.

expectedPoints.label: Defines authority per fact
expectedPoints.description: Separates authority for booking status, payment settlement, and provider transaction identifiers.

expectedPoints.label: Matches records reliably
expectedPoints.description: Uses stable booking, payment, provider, and idempotency identifiers rather than loose timestamps.

expectedPoints.label: Classifies repair actions
expectedPoints.description: Automatically fixes known safe states and routes ambiguous or financial cases to manual review.

expectedPoints.label: Measures and alerts on mismatches
expectedPoints.description: Tracks mismatch count, age, resolution result, and recurring causes.

followUps.prompt: Which mismatch would you never repair automatically?

hu.title: Booking és payment state reconciliation
hu.prompt: A booking database CONFIRMED állapotot mutat, a payment provider szerint viszont a payment failed. Hogyan terveznéd meg a reconciliationt, és melyik oldal lenne authoritative?
hu.answerStructureHint: Definiáld az authorityt factenként és a matching key-t, detektáld a mismatch-et, válaszd szét a safe repairt és manual reviewt, majd mérd az outcome-ot.

data_rec_authority.label: Factenként meghatározza az authorityt
data_rec_authority.description: Külön kezeli a booking status, payment settlement és provider transaction identifier ownershipjét.

data_rec_match.label: Megbízhatóan párosítja a recordokat
data_rec_match.description: Stable booking, payment, provider és idempotency identifier kerül elő, nem laza timestamp.

data_rec_action.label: Osztályozza a repair actiont
data_rec_action.description: Known safe state automatikusan javítható, ambiguous vagy financial eset manual review.

data_rec_metrics.label: Méri és alerteli a mismatch-et
data_rec_metrics.description: Mismatch count, age, resolution result és recurring cause látható.

followUps.data_rec_follow_auto: Melyik mismatch-et nem javítanád soha automatikusan?

questions.title: Deciding what belongs in a stored procedure
questions.prompt: A legacy Oracle system contains business logic in PL/SQL procedures. Which logic would you keep in the database, which would you move to application code, and how would bind variables fit?
questions.answerStructureHint: Separate data-centric set operations from cross-service orchestration, preserve contracts, and explain parameterization.

expectedPoints.label: Keeps cohesive data-centric work
expectedPoints.description: Keeps set-based, performance-sensitive, or consistency-critical data operations when they have a clear database contract.

expectedPoints.label: Moves orchestration to application code
expectedPoints.description: Moves cross-service workflows and logic that needs normal application testing, deployment, and ownership.

expectedPoints.label: Plans compatibility
expectedPoints.description: Uses contract tests, staged rollout, and compatibility when procedure signatures or callers change.

expectedPoints.label: Uses bind variables
expectedPoints.description: Separates SQL values from structure for injection protection and statement reuse.

followUps.prompt: Why would moving every stored procedure into application code also be risky?

hu.title: Stored procedure és application logic határa
hu.prompt: Egy legacy Oracle rendszer business logikát tartalmaz PL/SQL procedure-ökben. Mit hagynál a database-ben, mit vinnél application code-ba, és hogyan kapcsolódnak ide a bind variable-ök?
hu.answerStructureHint: Válaszd szét a data-centric set operationt és cross-service orchestrationt, őrizd a contractot, majd magyarázd el a parameterizationt.

data_sp_keep.label: Megtartja a cohesive data-centric munkát
data_sp_keep.description: Clear database contract mögött maradhat set-based, performance-sensitive vagy consistency-critical data operation.

data_sp_move.label: Application code-ba viszi az orchestrationt
data_sp_move.description: Cross-service workflow és normál application testinget, deploymentet és ownershipöt igénylő logika kikerül.

data_sp_migrate.label: Kompatibilis migrationt tervez
data_sp_migrate.description: Contract test, staged rollout és caller compatibility szerepel procedure signature change-nél.

data_sp_bind.label: Bind variable-öket használ
data_sp_bind.description: A SQL value és structure külön marad injection protection és statement reuse miatt.

followUps.data_sp_follow_all: Miért lenne kockázatos minden stored procedure-t automatikusan application code-ba költöztetni?

questions.title: Designing a fast dashboard read model
questions.prompt: A dashboard repeatedly aggregates millions of transactional rows and has become slow. How would you decide between better SQL, a materialized view, and a denormalized read model?
questions.answerStructureHint: Measure the current query, define freshness needs, then compare query tuning with precomputation and its consistency cost.

expectedPoints.label: Measures the current bottleneck
expectedPoints.description: Starts with execution plans, data volume, query frequency, and result shape before adding a new model.

expectedPoints.label: Defines freshness requirements
expectedPoints.description: Determines whether the dashboard needs immediate, seconds-old, or scheduled data.

expectedPoints.label: Compares the options
expectedPoints.description: Considers indexes or SQL rewrite first, then materialized or event-driven read models for repeated expensive work.

expectedPoints.label: Defines source of truth and repair
expectedPoints.description: Keeps transactional data authoritative and plans refresh, monitoring, and rebuild for the derived model.

followUps.prompt: When would a materialized view be the wrong choice even if it makes the query fast?

hu.title: Gyors dashboard read model tervezése
hu.prompt: Egy dashboard folyamatosan több millió transactional row-t aggregál, és belassult. Hogyan döntenél jobb SQL, materialized view és denormalized read model között?
hu.answerStructureHint: Először mérd a queryt és definiáld a freshness igényt, majd hasonlítsd össze a query tuningot és precomputation consistency költségét.

data_rm_measure.label: Megméri a jelenlegi bottlenecket
data_rm_measure.description: Execution plan, data volume, query frequency és result shape alapján indul.

data_rm_fresh.label: Definiálja a freshness igényt
data_rm_fresh.description: Kideríti, immediate, néhány másodperces vagy scheduled adat kell-e.

data_rm_options.label: Összehasonlítja az opciókat
data_rm_options.description: Index és SQL rewrite után materialized vagy event-driven read modelt mérlegel az ismétlődő drága munkára.

data_rm_owner.label: Meghatározza a source of truth-t és repairt
data_rm_owner.description: A transactional data authoritative marad, a derived modelhez refresh, monitoring és rebuild tartozik.

followUps.data_rm_follow_bad: Mikor lenne rossz választás a materialized view akkor is, ha gyorssá teszi a queryt?
